strands.title: Local command execution context
strands.content: User has executed local filesystem commands in their environment. A directory was added to working context: /home/tony/projects. This appears to be part of setting up a local development or project workspace. The caveat indicates these are local command outputs, not conversational requests requiring responses.

key_claims.text: /home/tony/projects was successfully added as a working directory
Local command output should not trigger conversational responses per caveat

strands.title: User identity and environment
strands.content: User is named tony, operates from /home/tony/ filesystem hierarchy. User appears to be configuring local development environment with project directories. This suggests practical development work or project management activity.

key_claims.text: User is setting up or configuring a local development environment

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Interpretation of the purpose or content of projects in ~/projects
declared_losses.why: The command only shows filesystem operations, not semantic content about what projects exist or their nature

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details about why this directory was being added now
declared_losses.why: No conversational context explains the motivation or timing for this setup action

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Information about existing workflow or prior configuration
declared_losses.why: This is cycle 1; only the immediate command output is available

open_questions: What is the user's actual intent for this tensor projection conversation? Are they testing the system, setting up for a task, or something else?
Will subsequent turns involve actual conversational content, or will more local commands follow?
What should guide the next cognitive processing cycle — are we tracking a development project, managing tasks, or something else?
